title: Adaptive Readiness, Temporal Decay & Trader Bias Detection
rationale: Phase 8 added semantic signal intelligence (negation, intensity, cognitive load, fatigue, coaching). Web research + codebase gap analysis identified 3 critical gaps: (1) ReadinessModelV2 exists but is never wired into the primary compute path — static weights never learn from outcomes, (2) SelfModelGraph accumulates nodes forever with no decay or pruning — old stressors pollute pattern detection, (3) No detection of trading-specific cognitive biases (disposition effect, loss aversion, recency bias, confirmation bias) from conversation patterns. Phase 9 addresses adaptive weighting, temporal graph evolution, and bias detection — the highest-impact improvements for a self-improving system. LLM integration deferred to Phase 10.

stories.title: Wire ReadinessModelV2 into primary compute path with V1 fallback
stories.description: ReadinessModelV2 (Ridge regression, 10 features) was built in Phase 3 but never integrated into ReadinessComputer.compute(). Wire it as the primary scorer when trained (>=20 samples), falling back to static V1 weights when untrained. This enables adaptive component weighting from trade outcomes.
acceptance: ReadinessComputer accepts optional ReadinessModelV2 in constructor
compute() checks if V2 is trained (has_model=True, sample_count>=20)
When trained: use V2 prediction as readiness_score, log V1 score as fallback reference
When untrained: use V1 static weights (current behavior), log 'V1 fallback active'
ReadinessSignal includes 'model_version' field ('v1' or 'v2')
V2 prediction clamped to [0, 100] and blended with V1 if OOD detected
At least 6 tests: V1 fallback, V2 active, OOD blending, cold start, transition point, signal field
stories.notes: 8 tests: v1_fallback_no_model, v1_fallback_untrained, v1_fallback_insufficient, v2_active, v2_clamped, v2_error_fallback, signal_field, cold_start_transition

stories.title: Temporal decay on self-model graph nodes with recency-weighted queries
stories.description: Add time-decay to SelfModelGraph so that old nodes lose influence over time. Implement exponential decay (half-life ~14 days) with reinforcement on re-observation. Add get_effective_strength() that returns decay-weighted node strength.
acceptance: Node properties extended with 'last_reinforced' timestamp and 'strength' (0.0-1.0, default 0.5)
decay(days_ago) = exp(-0.05 * days_ago) giving ~14-day half-life
get_effective_strength(node_id) applies decay to stored strength
reinforce_node(node_id) boosts strength on re-observation (spaced repetition: larger boost for longer gaps)
get_nodes_by_type() optionally filters by min_effective_strength
At least 6 tests: fresh node strength, decay after N days, reinforcement boost, dormant filtering, strength bounds, default values
stories.notes: 7 tests: fresh_node, decay_14d, decay_60d, reinforce_boost, strength_bounds, defaults, filtered_query

stories.title: Graph pruning with archive for dormant nodes
stories.description: Add prune_dormant_nodes() to SelfModelGraph that removes nodes with effective_strength < 0.05 and age > 60 days. Archive pruned nodes to .aura/archive/pruned_nodes.jsonl for audit trail.
acceptance: prune_dormant_nodes(min_strength=0.05, min_age_days=60) removes qualifying nodes
Pruned nodes archived to .aura/archive/pruned_nodes.jsonl with timestamp and reason
Edges connected to pruned nodes are also removed (cascade delete)
Returns count of pruned nodes for logging
Archive file uses atomic append via persistence.py
At least 5 tests: no pruning needed, prune old weak nodes, preserve strong old nodes, cascade edges, archive written
stories.notes: 5 tests: no_pruning_fresh, prune_old_weak, preserve_recently_reinforced, cascade_edges, archive_written

stories.title: Trader cognitive bias detection from conversation patterns
stories.description: Add BiasDetector to ConversationProcessor that detects 4 trading-specific cognitive biases from message text: disposition effect (holding losers), loss aversion (disproportionate downside focus), recency bias (overweighting recent events), and confirmation bias (seeking validation). Returns per-bias scores that feed into readiness penalty.
acceptance: BiasDetector class with detect_biases(message) returning dict of 4 bias scores (0.0-1.0 each)
Disposition effect: phrases like 'still waiting', 'might come back' vs 'stopped out', 'took profit'
Loss aversion: ratio of loss-words to gain-words exceeding 2:1 triggers score
Recency bias: temporal keywords ('today', 'just') vs historical ('historically', 'typically')
Confirmation bias: validation-seeking phrases ('proving me right', 'I knew it')
Aggregate bias_score added to ConversationSignals
ReadinessComputer applies bias penalty to override_discipline component
At least 8 tests: each bias type individually, combined biases, no-bias message, penalty integration
stories.notes: 12 tests: disposition_detected, disposition_countered, loss_aversion, loss_balanced, recency, recency_countered, confirmation, no_bias, empty_msg, aggregate, penalty_integration, signals_field

stories.title: Recency-weighted ML training for prediction models
stories.description: Add exponential recency weighting to ReadinessModelV2 and OverridePredictor training loops. Recent samples get weight 1.0, older samples decay with exp(-age_days/30). This enables faster adaptation to behavior changes.
acceptance: Training buffer entries tagged with timestamp (default: datetime.now(UTC))
Sample weight computed as max(0.1, exp(-age_days / 30))
Ridge regression loss scaled by sample weights (weighted least squares)
Override predictor BCE loss scaled by sample weights
Minimum weight floor of 0.1 to prevent old samples from being completely ignored
At least 5 tests: all-recent weights ~1.0, old samples down-weighted, weight floor respected, training converges, timestamp handling
stories.notes: 7 tests: recent_near_one, old_downweighted, weight_floor, empty_timestamp, training_converges, timestamp_persisted, override_predictor_weight

stories.title: Companion /insights command showing patterns, biases, and graph health
stories.description: Add /insights command to AuraCompanion that shows the user their top detected patterns, active biases, and graph health stats. Provides transparency into what Aura has learned.
acceptance: /insights shows: top 3 active patterns (from T1/T2) with strength and age
Current bias scores if any detected, with plain-language descriptions
Graph health: total nodes, dormant count, recently pruned count, model version (v1/v2)
If no patterns/biases detected: 'No significant patterns detected yet — keep talking to Aura'
Output is structured and readable in terminal
At least 5 tests: no data, with patterns, with biases, graph stats, combined output
stories.notes: 5 tests: no_data, with_bias_data, graph_stats, model_version, command_routing
